identified_risks.description: The migration to microservices may exceed the planned 18-month timeline due to unforeseen complexities.
The project may exceed the allocated budget of 2M USD due to additional resource requirements or unexpected challenges.
The team may not have the capacity to handle parallel development of multiple microservices, leading to delays.
Unexpected regulatory changes could impact the migration process and require additional compliance efforts.

critical_assumptions.statement: The development team has the necessary skills and resources to manage parallel development of microservices.
There will be no significant regulatory changes during the migration period that could affect the project.

failure_modes.description: Integration complexities arise when transitioning from monolithic to microservices architecture.
failure_modes.trigger_conditions: Increased inter-service communication and data consistency issues during migration.

failure_modes.description: Insufficient resources or expertise to manage multiple microservices development leads to project delays.
failure_modes.trigger_conditions: High workload and lack of skilled personnel during the migration.

mitigation_ideas.suggestion: Implement agile methodologies to allow for iterative progress and adjustments to the timeline.
Establish a contingency budget to accommodate potential overruns and unexpected costs.
Conduct a skills assessment and provide training or hire additional resources to support parallel development.
Regularly review regulatory landscape and engage with compliance experts to anticipate changes.

unknowns: The risks and assumptions were derived from the migration plan details, focusing on operational and financial aspects. Implicit assumptions about team capacity and regulatory stability were identified as critical to the project's success.
